title: Action
description: An action performed by an agent.
$comment:
This is an implementation of schema.org
[`Action`](https://schema.org/Action).

In Stencila Schema it provides a neutral activity/event node for provenance
graphs. Actions can be connected to input and output resources using
`GraphEdge` relationships, while retaining schema.org metadata such as the
agent, object, result, instrument, status, and timing.

The schema.org `object` and `result` properties are exposed as the plural
Stencila properties `objects` and `results` because an action can consume or
produce several resources or literal values. The singular schema.org property
names are retained as aliases.

More specific action types such as [`CreateAction`](./create-action.md),
[`ExecuteAction`](./execute-action.md), and
[`ConvertAction`](./convert-action.md) should be used when the activity kind
is known.

analogues.notes: Direct schema.org source type, used as the common base for provenance activities.

type.$comment: This is a special property analogous to JSON-LD's `@type` keyword.

id.$comment: This is a special property analogous to JSON-LD's `@id` keyword.

identifiers.description: Any kind of identifier for any kind of Thing.
identifiers.$comment:
Some identifiers have specific properties e.g the `issn` property for
the `Periodical` type. These should be used in preference to this
property which is intended for identifiers that do not yet have a
specific property. Identifiers can be represented as strings, but
using a `PropertyValue` will usually be better because it allows
for `propertyID` (i.e. the type of identifier).

actionStatus.description: The current status of the action.

agent.description: The direct performer or driver of the action.

participants.description: Other agents that participated in the action.

provider.description: The service provider, service operator, or performer responsible for the action.

objects.description: The objects or input values upon which the action is carried out.

results.description: The objects or values produced by the action.

instrument.description: The object, software, or other instrument that helped perform the action.

error.description: An error produced by the action.
